tasks.spec: Implement the core-contract-metadata lane from contracts/jac-3643-nate-native-upgrade.v1.json. Your current working directory IS an isolated Git worktree of the frozen source repository. Modify only ringer.py and tests/test_contract_metadata.py. Leave all changes uncommitted. Do not push, merge, restart services, call Paperclip, touch Beads, or change any other path. Required behavior: (1) add backwards-compatible optional run-level manifest fields risk, orchestrator, and contract_review; risk values are low, medium, high, critical, with absent treated as low; orchestrator is a string-keyed JSON object; contract_review is a string-keyed JSON object. Validate types and preserve these fields through Manifest.from_path and with_max_parallel. (2) Add a deterministic Manifest.contract_sha256 computed from UTF-8 JSON using sort_keys=true, separators comma/colon, ensure_ascii=false over exactly: run_name and an ordered tasks array containing each task key, spec, check, expect_files as a list, engine, model, task_type, and verified. JSON source key order, whitespace, workdir, max_parallel, paperclip_issue, bead_id, routine_id, goal_id, timeout, and review metadata must not change the hash. (3) Add high/critical lint findings unless contract_review has verdict PASS, reviewer_model, reviewer_family, reviewer_runtime, and contract_sha256 exactly matching Manifest.contract_sha256; also find same-family review when orchestrator.family equals reviewer_family. Low/medium runs remain compatible and need no review. (4) Expose risk, orchestrator, contract_review, and contract_sha256 in live/final state snapshots. Trace StateWriter construction and Runner wiring rather than creating a second state path. (5) Add risk, contract_sha256, orchestrator model/runtime, and reviewer model/runtime/verdict to attempt evidence in both structured row fields where safe and the notes trail so JSONL consumers retain them. Do not change PASS authority: executable checks still decide; do not add semantic override logic. (6) Preserve the existing exactly-two-attempt maximum and raw check output injection. Tests must prove old-manifest compatibility, canonical hash invariance to JSON key order and irrelevant metadata, hash sensitivity to semantic task fields, with_max_parallel preservation, high-risk missing/mismatched/same-family lint findings, valid cross-family PASS, state exposure, and attempt evidence. Follow local style and avoid broad refactors. End your response with CORE_CONTRACT_METADATA_DONE and a concise file/test summary.
tasks.check:
set -eu; git add -N tests/test_contract_metadata.py; changed=$(git status --porcelain | sed 's/^...//'); bad=$(printf '%s
' "$changed" | grep -Ev '^(ringer.py|tests/test_contract_metadata.py)$' || true); if [ -n "$bad" ]; then echo "FAIL: core worker changed paths outside ownership:"; printf '%s
' "$bad"; exit 1; fi; python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_contract_metadata.py || { echo 'FAIL: contract metadata tests failed'; exit 1; }; python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_lint.py tests/test_verify_order.py tests/test_model_log.py tests/test_launch_receipts.py || { echo 'FAIL: core regression tests failed'; exit 1; }; git diff --check || { echo 'FAIL: git diff check failed'; exit 1; }; mkdir -p /Users/hermes/.ringer/work/jac-3643/exports; git diff --binary -- ringer.py tests/test_contract_metadata.py > /Users/hermes/.ringer/work/jac-3643/exports/core-contract-metadata.patch; test -s /Users/hermes/.ringer/work/jac-3643/exports/core-contract-metadata.patch || { echo 'FAIL: exported core patch is empty'; exit 1; }; echo 'PASS: core contract metadata behavior, regressions, ownership, and patch export verified'
tasks.verified: contract metadata is backwards-compatible, canonically hashed, high-risk lint-gated, exposed in state/eval evidence, regression-tested, ownership-bounded, and exported as a non-empty patch

tasks.spec: Implement the policy-and-template lane from contracts/jac-3643-nate-native-upgrade.v1.json. Your current working directory IS an isolated Git worktree of the frozen source repository. Create or modify only docs/nate-native-fleet-policy.md, schema/ringer-contract-review.v1.json, templates/frontier-worker-loop/README.md, templates/frontier-worker-loop/manifest.json, and tests/test_frontier_worker_template.py. Leave all changes uncommitted. Do not push, merge, restart services, call Paperclip, touch Beads, or change any other path. Required content: (1) docs/nate-native-fleet-policy.md must define the corrected control loop: frontier orchestrator writes the immutable contract and executable checks; economical proven workers implement in isolated parallel lanes; executable checks decide PASS/FAIL; exactly one retry receives raw failure output; receipts drive per-task-type routing; semantic review is conditional and can veto green but never override red; Ringside is observational; Paperclip is trigger/telemetry/work-product projection; Beads/Vault remain authoritative. Include four modes: canonical, contract-reviewed high-risk, semantic-review, and bakeoff/exploration with a suggested 80-90 percent proven / 10-20 percent exploration policy and promotion only after roughly 20 comparable receipts. (2) schema/ringer-contract-review.v1.json must be a strict JSON Schema for a PASS/FAIL contract-review receipt including contract_sha256, reviewer provider/model/family/runtime, harness attestation, reviewed_at, requirement_gaps, required_changes, and advisories. (3) templates/frontier-worker-loop/manifest.json must be valid JSON and demonstrate risk/orchestrator/contract_review plus two independent economical worker tasks with task_type, model, meaningful checks that print failures, verified sentences, paperclip_issue, and bead_id placeholders. It must make contract_review.contract_sha256 a replacement placeholder and state that a high-risk run is not dispatchable before exact-manifest cross-family PASS. (4) the template README must provide a fill/freeze/hash/review/lint/run/integrate/semantic-review/receipt workflow and make authority boundaries explicit. (5) tests/test_frontier_worker_template.py must stay lane-local: validate the JSON/schema/template and policy invariants without importing or depending on the new ringer.py behavior from the other lane. Use only the standard library and pytest. End your response with POLICY_TEMPLATE_DONE and a concise file/test summary.
tasks.check:
set -eu; for f in docs/nate-native-fleet-policy.md schema/ringer-contract-review.v1.json templates/frontier-worker-loop/README.md templates/frontier-worker-loop/manifest.json tests/test_frontier_worker_template.py; do git add -N "$f"; done; changed=$(git status --porcelain | sed 's/^...//'); bad=$(printf '%s
' "$changed" | grep -Ev '^(docs/nate-native-fleet-policy.md|schema/ringer-contract-review.v1.json|templates/frontier-worker-loop/README.md|templates/frontier-worker-loop/manifest.json|tests/test_frontier_worker_template.py)$' || true); if [ -n "$bad" ]; then echo "FAIL: policy worker changed paths outside ownership:"; printf '%s
' "$bad"; exit 1; fi; python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_frontier_worker_template.py || { echo 'FAIL: policy/template tests failed'; exit 1; }; python3 -m json.tool schema/ringer-contract-review.v1.json >/dev/null || { echo 'FAIL: review schema is invalid JSON'; exit 1; }; python3 -m json.tool templates/frontier-worker-loop/manifest.json >/dev/null || { echo 'FAIL: template manifest is invalid JSON'; exit 1; }; git diff --check || { echo 'FAIL: git diff check failed'; exit 1; }; mkdir -p /Users/hermes/.ringer/work/jac-3643/exports; git diff --binary -- docs/nate-native-fleet-policy.md schema/ringer-contract-review.v1.json templates/frontier-worker-loop/README.md templates/frontier-worker-loop/manifest.json tests/test_frontier_worker_template.py > /Users/hermes/.ringer/work/jac-3643/exports/policy-and-template.patch; test -s /Users/hermes/.ringer/work/jac-3643/exports/policy-and-template.patch || { echo 'FAIL: exported policy patch is empty'; exit 1; }; echo 'PASS: policy, schema, template, lane-local tests, ownership, and patch export verified'
tasks.verified: the fleet policy, strict review schema, reusable template, lane-local tests, authority boundaries, ownership, and non-empty patch export are mechanically validated
